w:r.w:t: This is a brilliant economic dynamic. Having a dual-currency system—where
acts as the stable gold standard and

acts as a volatile, almost crypto-like chaotic commodity—creates massive narrative and mechanical friction for the players. Do they spend their D-Dust now while the exchange rate is high, or hoard it hoping it spikes before they hit the Black Market?
Combined with the grit of your Hedge-Charms and Holding Fees, this module needs to be part
Here is the developer-ready blueprint for
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 05: The Item & Inventory Foundry

A standalone Python microservice. It manages the mathematical definitions of every physical object in the world. It generates loot drops, calculates the volatile D-Dust exchange rate, calculates weapon/armor "Holding Fees" (Stamina/Focus locks), and outputs the exact mechanical result when a player uses a consumable or reads an info-item.
Pydantic (Polymorphic schema for the 7 item types)
Built-in RNG and volatility algorithms for the D-Dust market.
├── schemas.py # Pydantic schemas for the 7 item types + Wealth
├── gen_loot.py # Logic for dropping items from enemies/chests
└── effect_resolver.py # The math for applying Charms and Salves
This defines the exact DNA of your 7 item categories and your dual-economy.
# 2. ARMOR (Tied to the Stamina/Focus Holding Fees)
# If true, explodes on a Nat 1 throw
# e.g., "Advantage on History checks regarding the Elven Empire"
4. Internal Logic & Code Execution (How to Code It)
The GM App pings this module when players enter a new town or wait 24 hours to get the current D-Dust market value.
# Chaos level of the world increases the volatility of D-Dust
# Example: Base is 10 Aetherium. With high chaos, it might drop to 6 or spike to 14.
When a player uses a "Vigor Salt" or throws a "Cinder-Pot", this module does the math so the VTT doesn't have to.
Example Output A: The Loot Drop
When the Encounter Manager says a monster died, the Game Master App asks this module for the loot. Notice how D-Dust and Aetherium are split.
Example Output B: Info & Quest Items
When players loot a safe or a courier.
"Shipments of Aetherium routed through the Iron Caldera have been compromised by the Wolf Cult."
complete, look at the seamless integration of your entire data flow:
calculates that because it's a high-chaos zone, the 3 grams of D-Dust the Cultist drops is currently worth a massive
The player equips the Cinder-Pot to their
Every single piece of your intricate lore, 12-stat system, and economy is mathematically protected in its own isolated box.
If Batch 2 (The Entities) is locked in, we can move to the final pieces of the puzzle.
(Resolving your "Scars & Stars" dice logic) and the
(The Clash Contested Combat system). Shall we proceed?
